title: ROARMessage
description: ROAR Layer 4: Unified message format. One type for all agent communication, differentiated by intent.

id.description: Unique message identifier. Format: msg_<10-char hex>

to.description: Full identity of the intended receiver

intent.description: What the sender wants the receiver to do. Never use custom values — map to the closest intent.

payload.description: Message content. Structure depends on intent — see spec/04-exchange.md for intent-specific payload shapes.

context.description: Session context and metadata. Optional but recommended for tracing.

in_reply_to.description: ID of the message this is responding to

protocol.description: Original protocol if this message was adapted (e.g., 'a2a', 'mcp')

auth.description: Authentication. Set by .sign(), verified by .verify(). Absent on unsigned messages.

signature.description: Message signature. HMAC-SHA256 format: hmac-sha256:<64-char hex>. Ed25519 format: ed25519:<base64url>.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp when signature was created. Used for replay protection.

public_key.description: Ed25519 signer public key (hex-encoded, 64 chars). Present only when the Ed25519 signing path is used; absent for HMAC-SHA256.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp (seconds since epoch, float) when the message was created.
